input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1C1C1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Spacing="16" Padding="20">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#FFFFFF}" />

        <Label Text="This page responds to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework reads `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and picks `Light` or `Dark` automatically. When the user toggles the OS theme, values update live — no event wiring needed.

---

### C# equivalent — `SetAppThemeColor` / `SetAppTheme<T>`

For colors there's a shorthand:

```csharp
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
//                                               ↑ light      ↑ dark
```

For any non-color property, use the generic version:

```csharp
var img = new Image();
img.SetAppTheme<string>(Image.SourceProperty, "sun.png", "moon.png");
```

---

### Key points

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Bound to `AppTheme.Light` / `AppTheme.Dark` from the OS |
| **Live update** | Yes — switches at runtime when the system theme changes |
| **Resource dictionaries** | You can combine this with `AppThemeBinding` inside `Style` setters in `App.xaml` to theme globally |
| **Force a theme** | `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark;` overrides the OS setting |

For a larger app, define themed colors as resources in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` — keeps your pages clean and your palette in one place.
